questions.question:
The following C function takes a single-linked list of integers as a parameter and rearranges the elements of the list. The function is called with the list containing the integers 1,2,3,4,5,6,7 in the given order. What will be the contents of the list after the function completes execution?

 struct node {
   int value;
   struct node *next;
};
Void rearrange(struct node *list) {
   struct node *p, * q;
   int temp;
   if(!list|| !list-> next) return;
   p=list; q=list->next;
   while(q)
  {
     temp = p->value;
     p->value = q->value;
     q->value = temp;
     p = q->next;
     q = p?p->next:0;
  }
}


(GATE CSE 2008)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
The following C function takes a singly-linked list of integers as a parameter and rearranges the elements of the list. The list is represented as pointer to a structure. The function is called with the list containing the integers 1,2,3,4,5,6,7 in the given order. What will be the contents of the list after the function completes execution? 

 struct node {int value; struct node *next;);
void rearrange (struct node *list) {
    struct node *p, *q;
    int temp;
    if (!list || !list -> next) return;
    p = list; q = list -> next;
    while (q) {
        temp = p -> value;
        p -> value = q -> value;
        q -> value = temp;
        p = q -> next;
        q = p ? p -> next : 0;
      }
}


(GATE IT 2005)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the function f defined below.


struct item
{
    int data;
    struct item * next;
};
int f(struct item *p)
{
    return ((p == NULL) || (p->next == NULL) ||
            ((P->data <= p->next->data) &&
            f(p->next)));
}
For a given linked list p, the function f returns 1 if and only if

(GATE CSE 2003)
options:
A.

the list is empty or has exactly one element

B.

the elements in the list are sorted in non-decreasing order of data value

C.

the elements in the list are sorted in non-increasing order of data value

D.

not all elements in the list have the same data value
questions.correct_answer:
B.

the elements in the list are sorted in non-decreasing order of data value
